originalTitle: ‘I’m taking the big one’: Noskova says sight of trophies inspired Wimbledon triumph
summary: Linda Noskova said seeing the trophy sitting by Centre Court helped her refocus at a key time, and Karolina Muchova said she would use the defeat as motivation
content: A jubilant Linda Noskova said seeing the Wimbledon trophy sitting by Centre Court helped her reset at a vital time as she clinched her first grand slam title with a dramatic 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 victory over her fellow Czech Karolina Muchova at Wimbledon. The 21-year-old led 6-2, 5-2 only to miss five match points as Muchova levelled but Noskova took a bathroom break and reset brilliantly to become the sixth Czech woman to lift the title in the Open era. “What really helped me, the first step I took off court, the trophies were there,” Noskova said. “I was like: ‘I’m not going to take the small one, I’m taking the big one. I have been so close. This will probably be the heartbreak of my life.’ I just kind of started to focus on myself all over again, which was the key point.” Noskova said the first game of the final set, when she saved three break points, was the crucial moment in the match. “Let’s say I’m brave enough to say that the third set would not have been the same if maybe I would have lost the first game,” she said. “I lost five games in a row in the second one, so it was very, very important to start off in a good way in the third one.” Noskova would rather have finished it off in two sets but was proud of her resilience. “I feel like if I would have saved the spectators, my team or myself the stress and all the heart-stopping moments, I would say that that would be better. Just winning it this way, really having to fight for it, having all these ups and downs, it matters a lot, but I do have to learn a lot from this match, definitely.” Muchova said it was “one of my worst matches of the tournament” and revealed that the effort of levelling the match left her almost spent. “She played really fast and I was kind of looking for myself,” she said. “It took a lot of power and strength out of me, I would say, to get back in that second set. I gave it my all.” The 29-year-old, whose only previous slam final was the 2023 French Open she lost to Iga Swiatek, tried to emphasise the positives. “It will definitely take me a few days,” she said. “But I was in the finals. It’s still a pretty great achievement. It’s still something I would take before the tournament. “I think my game is good. I’m improving. I feel good on the court. Yeah, it’s a dream of mine, it’s a goal of mine to lift that grand slam trophy. This is a setback, but as well a motivation.”
chineseTitle: “我要拿下一个大奖杯”：诺斯科娃表示看到奖杯激励了温布尔登的胜利
chineseSummary: 琳达·诺斯科娃 (Linda Noskova) 表示，看到奖杯坐落在中央球场，帮助她在关键时刻重新集中注意力，卡罗琳娜·穆乔娃 (Karolina Muchova) 表示，她会将这次失败作为动力
chineseContent: 欣喜若狂的琳达·诺斯科娃 (Linda Noskova) 表示，看到温布尔登奖杯坐落在中央球场，她在关键时刻重新调整了心态，在温布尔登以 6-2、5-7、6-3 戏剧性地战胜了捷克同胞卡罗琳娜·穆乔娃 (Karolina Muchova)，夺得了自己的第一个大满贯冠军。这位21岁的选手以6比2和5比2领先，但在穆乔娃扳平比分后错失了五个赛点，但诺斯科娃在洗手间休息后出色地重新调整，成为公开赛时代第六位夺得冠军的捷克女性。 “真正对我有帮助的是，我走出球场的第一步，奖杯就在那里，”诺斯科娃说。 “我当时的反应是：‘我不会选择小的，我要选择大的。我已经非常接近了。这可能会是我一生的心碎。’我只是开始重新关注自己，这是关键点。”诺斯科娃表示，决胜盘第一局她挽救了三个破发点，这是比赛的关键时刻。 “假设我有足够的勇气说，如果我可能输掉第一局，第三盘就不会一样了，”她说。 “第二场比赛我连输了五场比赛，所以第三场比赛以良好的开局非常非常重要。”诺斯科娃宁愿以两盘结束比赛，但她为自己的韧性感到自豪。 “我觉得如果我能让观众、我的团队或我自己免受压力和所有令人心碎的时刻，我会说那会更好。以这种方式赢得比赛，真的必须为之奋斗，经历所有这些起起落落，这很重要，但我确实必须从这场比赛中学到很多东西，绝对的。”穆乔娃表示，这是“我在本届锦标赛中最糟糕的一场比赛”，并透露，扳平比赛的努力让她几乎耗尽了精力。 “她打得很快，我在寻找自己，”她说。 “花了很多精力
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
